title: Checks
description:
Checks contain their value written both numerically and as words. Write a program to read a positive dollar amount less than a trillion, with two decimal places, and display the amount in words.

Here is the output for 64325.28:

SIXTY FOUR THOUSAND THREE HUNDRED TWENTY FIVE AND TWENTY EIGHT/100 DOLLARS

Notice that the word "AND" only appears before the hundredths. Spelling counts! Traditional American counting rules apply.
input: A single line containing a dollar amount less than a trillion dollars.
output: A single line with the word representation of the number exactly as patterned here.

samples.output: ONE HUNDRED TWENTY THREE AND FORTY FIVE/100 DOLLARS

source: Olympiad, USA Computing Olympiad, 1998-1999 Season, USACO Winter 1999 Contest, Division B
